item.title: US military completes 12th straight night of strikes on Iran
item.description:
By Kanishka Singh

WASHINGTON, July 22 (Reuters) - The U.S. military said on Wednesday it completed its latest round of strikes on Iran at President Donald Trump's direction, for a 12th successive night of American attacks.

Here are details and quotes:

• "U.S. forces struck Iranian military targets including maritime capabilities, missile and drone storage facilities, coastal surveillance sites, and air defense assets," the U.S. Central Command said in a statement.

item.title: Top diplomats gather for security forum in Southeast Asia
item.description:
By Mikhail Flores

MANILA, July 23 (Reuters) - Top diplomats from Asia, Europe and North America gathered on Thursday for the security-focused ASEAN Regional Forum in Manila, where ministers were expected to issue calls for a peaceful settlement after renewed and intensifying hostilities in the Middle East.

item.title: US announces civilian nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia
item.description: The United States on Wednesday announced a landmark deal with Saudi Arabia that would establish a civilian nuclear program in the Gulf country, with Washington insisting the agreement includes nonproliferation safeguards. Washington has sought in recent years to include any deal concerning a nuclear agreement in a wider strategic effort that would see Saudi Arabia normalize ties with Israel, along with the signing of a US-Saudi defense pact.

item.title: US says it reaches nuclear power deal with Saudi Arabia
item.description:
By Timothy Gardner

July 22 (Reuters) - The U.S. and Saudi Arabia have reached a landmark agreement on civil nuclear power, the U.S. Energy Department said on Wednesday, a deal that allows the kingdom to build nuclear reactors using American technology and to enrich uranium.

A civil nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia has been in the works for years during both President Donald Trump's first administration and that of former President Joe Biden.

item.title: US announces civilian nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia
item.description: The United States on Wednesday announced a landmark deal with Saudi Arabia that would establish a civilian nuclear program in the Gulf country, with Washington insisting the agreement includes safeguards against non-proliferation. Washington has sought in recent years to include any deal concerning a nuclear agreement in a wider strategic play that would see Saudi Arabia normalize ties with Israel, along with the signing of a US defense pact.

item.title: US, Saudi Arabia sign landmark nuclear power agreement
item.description: The multibillion-dollar agreement will now undergo congressional review, with expected provisions on uranium enrichment likely to draw scrutiny from some lawmakers.

item.title: US, Saudi Arabia sign landmark nuclear power agreement
item.description: The multibillion-dollar agreement will now undergo congressional review, with expected provisions on uranium enrichment likely to draw scrutiny from some lawmakers.

item.title: US, Saudi Arabia sign landmark nuclear power agreement
item.description: The multibillion-dollar agreement will now undergo congressional review, with expected provisions on uranium enrichment likely to draw scrutiny from some lawmakers.

item.title: US, Saudi Arabia sign landmark nuclear power agreement
item.description: The multibillion-dollar agreement will now undergo congressional review, with expected provisions on uranium enrichment likely to draw scrutiny from some lawmakers.

item.title: Why New York's Mamdani cannot have Israel's Netanyahu arrested
item.description:
(In paragraph 11, corrects first name of Harvard Law School professor to Alex, not Alexander)

By Luc Cohen

NEW YORK, July 22 (Reuters) - New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani said this week that he had determined he lacked the legal authority to have Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu arrested for alleged war crimes should he visit the largest U.S. city, after earlier saying he was considering the move.

item.title: Exclusive-Iran strikes on CIA facilities prompt questions about possible Russian role
item.description:
By Jonathan Landay, Gram Slattery and Timour Azhari

WASHINGTON/RIYADH, July 22 (Reuters) - Iranian drone attacks on CIA facilities in the Gulf have prompted U.S. intelligence analysts to investigate whether Russia assisted by providing targeting information or advanced drone technology, said four people familiar with U.S. intelligence.

item.title: US-Iran strikes: latest developments
item.description: Here are the latest main events related to the war in the Middle East. Any aggression against Iran, including our infrastructure, will compel a powerful and decisive response," Araghchi said on X. - Trump threatens payback - Trump threatened to knock out one Iranian bridge or power plant in return for each attack on shipping in the Strait of Hormuz, warning he was "not finished" with a war that has already cost Washington $37.5 billion.

item.title: Turkey to begin surveys for planned northern Cyprus gas pipeline
item.description: The planned 63-mile link is intended to supply gas for electricity generation but has drawn condemnation from the Republic of Cyprus amid rising rivalries in the eastern Mediterranean.

item.title: Turkey to begin surveys for planned northern Cyprus gas pipeline
item.description: The planned 63-mile link is intended to supply gas for electricity generation but has drawn condemnation from the Republic of Cyprus amid rising rivalries in the eastern Mediterranean.

item.title: Turkey to begin surveys for planned northern Cyprus gas pipeline
item.description: The planned 63-mile link is intended to supply gas for electricity generation but has drawn condemnation from the Republic of Cyprus amid rising rivalries in the eastern Mediterranean.

item.title: Saudi nuclear deal delivers strategic economic prizes as Trump boosts Gulf allies
item.description: A landmark US-Saudi civilian nuclear cooperation agreement is poised to deliver billion-dollar contracts and many question marks as the Trump administration seeks to anchor Riyadh in America's commercial orbit.

item.title: Saudi nuclear deal delivers strategic economic prizes as Trump boosts Gulf allies
item.description: A landmark US-Saudi civilian nuclear cooperation agreement is poised to deliver billion-dollar contracts and many question marks as the Trump administration seeks to anchor Riyadh in America's commercial orbit.

item.description: A landmark US-Saudi civilian nuclear cooperation agreement is poised to deliver billion-dollar contracts and many question marks as the Trump administration seeks to anchor Riyadh in America's commercial orbit.
